title: A resolution expressing the sense of the Senate that the United States shall not deploy United States military assets or personnel to Gaza for purposes of "taking over" Gaza.
plain_summary: This Senate resolution says that the United States should not send soldiers or military equipment to take control of Gaza. The resolution also says America should not spend money or risk American lives to take over Gaza. The senators believe that taking control of Gaza would hurt America's interests. Congress has not given permission for the military to do this kind of action in Gaza.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S821)

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses the Senate's opinion that the United States should not send American military forces or equipment to Gaza to take control of that territory.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: The Senate recognizes these important facts. On October seventh, two thousand twenty three, the terrorist group Hamas attacked Israel and killed one thousand one hundred ninety five Israelis. Israeli military actions in Gaza have resulted in more than forty five thousand Palestinian deaths. On January fifteenth, two thousand twenty five, Israel and Hamas agreed to stop fighting. This ceasefire led to the return of innocent people held hostage by Hamas, stopped Israeli military actions in Gaza, moved Israeli forces away from Gaza, and created a chance for other countries to send aid to Palestinians who need help. On February fourth, two thousand twenty five, President Trump suggested that the United States would take over the Gaza Strip and force its people to move somewhere else. Palestinian people have the right to make their own decisions about their government and to have their own independent country. They also have the right to live with dignity and in peace with their neighbors. If the United States took control of Gaza, it would damage relationships with American partners in the Middle East and around the world. It would prove that Iran's harmful statements are correct and would cause more people to become violent terrorists. This would create greater threats to American interests and to American citizens and military members in the region. The American people do not want a new long military mission in the Middle East or another endless war there. Americans do not want to spend countless billions of tax dollars on American military operations in Gaza or to force two million people to leave Gaza. There is no permission from Congress, which is required by law, for using American military force in Gaza.

sections.title: Senate's Position
sections.text: Therefore, the Senate believes that the United States will not send American Armed Forces to Gaza, will not put American lives at risk, and will not spend American money to achieve the stated goal of taking over Gaza.
